text.title: AI Debt Detection Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Debt Detection application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Detector MUST implement at least 4 signals: shadow names (function shadowing built-in or earlier symbol), abandoned variables (assigned but never read), wrapper-on-wrapper (function that only calls another), cross-file duplication (>=80% token overlap). Subsets are rejected.
rule.rationale: Each signal catches a different agent failure mode; covering <4 misses common debt classes and the report under-represents the problem.

rule.statement: Each signal MUST carry an explicit threshold (e.g. duplication >= 80%, abandoned vars >= 3 per file). Threshold-less detectors are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without thresholds, every minor coincidence triggers; the report becomes noise and the team learns to ignore it.

rule.statement: Findings MUST be ranked low/medium/high using a declared rubric (e.g. abandoned var in test = low, wrapper-on-wrapper in payment path = high). Flat lists are rejected.
rule.rationale: Triage requires ranking; without it, fixers attack the visible items not the costly items.

rule.statement: Each finding MUST carry a remediation route: inline fix (small), refactor ticket (larger), convention update (recurring). Findings without routes are rejected.
rule.rationale: Findings without next steps stagnate; routes turn the report into a backlog generator.

rule.statement: Detector MUST run at least weekly; report stored alongside the team's debt log. One-off scans are rejected.
rule.rationale: AI debt accrues continuously; one-off scans miss the trend; cadence makes the report a leading indicator.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
